task_summary: Design and implement a simple event bus system in Python that provides decoupled communication between components through a publish-subscribe pattern. The system will be thread-safe, in-memory, and synchronous, suitable for lightweight applications that need event-driven architecture without the complexity of distributed systems.
chosen_approach: Memory Push-Based Bus with explicit subscription and error isolation. This approach provides simplicity, low latency, and reliability for a single-process Python application while avoiding the complexity of persistence, async handling, or hierarchical routing.

components.name: Event
components.responsibility: Container for event data including type, payload, and unique identifier

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Initialize event with type, payload, and id

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description: Arbitrary data to carry with the event

functions.notes: Uses uuid4 for auto-generation if id not provided

components.name: SimpleEventBus
components.responsibility: Core orchestrator that manages event subscription, publishing, and listener lifecycle

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Initialize the event bus with internal state

functions.name: subscribe
functions.description: Register a listener callback for a specific event type

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description: The event type to listen for

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Function to call when event is published

functions.name: unsubscribe
functions.description: Remove a specific listener from an event type

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description: The event type to unsubscribe from

functions.name: publish
functions.description: Publish an event to all registered listeners
functions.notes: Catches exceptions per listener to prevent cascade failures

functions.name: clear
functions.description: Remove all listeners from the bus
functions.notes: Useful for testing and resetting state

shared_data_structures: dict[str, list[Callable]] - Maps event types to listener callbacks
threading.Lock - Ensures thread-safe access to listener lists
edge_cases: Listener raises an exception - must be caught to prevent blocking other listeners
Unsubscribing a callback that was never added - should be handled gracefully
Duplicate subscriptions to same event type - should be allowed (multiple listeners)
Publishing to event type with no listeners - should be a no-op
Thread safety when subscribe/publish/unsubscribe called concurrently
assumptions: The event bus is used within a single Python process
All listeners are synchronous callables (no async/await)
Thread safety is required via explicit locking
Event types are strings and used as dictionary keys
Python 3.10+ with standard library only (no external dependencies)
moe_routing_context: Python 3.10+ standard library, Observer pattern, thread-safe design with threading.Lock, dataclasses for type safety, callable protocol for listener functions, basic error handling with try-except blocks

dropped_ideas.idea: Persistent Pull-Based event bus with database storage
Hierarchical Bus with distributed event federation
